narrative: You push through a hedgerow and notice something clinging to your sleeve...
interesting_fact: Cleavers is said to be one of the inspirations for Velcro — the inventor George de Mestral studied how the hooked hairs clung to fabric and fur.

questions.question: What plant is this?
What is the common name of this plant?
Does Cleavers have any other common names? Select all that apply
What is the botanical name of Cleavers?
Where is Cleavers typically found?
options: Hedgerows, woodland edges and disturbed ground
Shaded woodland floors near tree roots
questions.correct: Hedgerows, woodland edges and disturbed ground

questions.question: Name a key identifying feature of Cleavers
options: Square stems covered in tiny hooked hairs that cling to clothing
Round hollow stems with a strong smell
Thick waxy leaves with purple undersides
Heart shaped leaves in groups of three
questions.correct: Square stems covered in tiny hooked hairs that cling to clothing

questions.question: Name another key identifying feature of Cleavers
options: Narrow leaves arranged in whorls of 6-8 around the stem
Broad oval leaves with serrated edges
Large palmate leaves with five lobes
Tiny round leaves growing in opposite pairs
questions.correct: Narrow leaves arranged in whorls of 6-8 around the stem

questions.question: What is Cleavers used for? Select all that apply
options: Young shoots added to soups and stews
Made into a tea or cold infusion
Seeds dried as a coffee substitute
Eaten raw in large quantities as a salad
correct: Young shoots added to soups and stews
Made into a tea or cold infusion
Seeds dried as a coffee substitute

questions.question: What does Cleavers taste like?
What are the risks or cautions with Cleavers? Select all that apply
options: Avoid if pregnant — can stimulate uterine contractions
Avoid if on blood thinning medication
Only eat young spring growth — older stems are tough and unpleasant
Avoid once gone to seed in late summer
Toxic to humans in any quantity
correct: Avoid if pregnant — can stimulate uterine contractions
Avoid if on blood thinning medication
Only eat young spring growth — older stems are tough and unpleasant
Avoid once gone to seed in late summer

questions.question: Which species could Cleavers be confused with?
When does Cleavers flower?
What time of year is Cleavers best harvested?
options: Early spring when shoots are young and tender
Autumn after it has gone to seed
Winter when other plants have died back
questions.correct: Early spring when shoots are young and tender

questions.question: Is Cleavers native to the UK?
options: It was introduced in the 18th century

questions.question: What was Cleavers historically used to treat? Select all that apply
How was Cleavers applied medicinally? Select all that apply
options: As a tea or cold infusion
Applied as a poultice to wounds and skin
Ground into a powder and swallowed dry
correct: As a tea or cold infusion
Applied as a poultice to wounds and skin

questions.question: What is the life cycle of Cleavers?
options: Biennial — takes two years to complete
Ephemeral — only lasts a few weeks

questions.question: What soil or habitat conditions does Cleavers prefer?
options: Dry sandy soil in full sun

questions.question: What plant family does Cleavers belong to?
What is the origin of the name Galium?
options: From the Greek for milk — a related plant was used to curdle milk in cheesemaking
Named after the Roman general Galius
From the Latin word for sticky
From the Celtic word for hedgerow
questions.correct: From the Greek for milk — a related plant was used to curdle milk in cheesemaking

questions.question: What does Aparine mean in the botanical name?
